title: Atlas "From Altai to the Caspian Sea" — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
Atlas “From Altai to the Caspian Sea”
Atlas of monuments, sights of history, culture and geography “From Altai to the Caspian Sea” is a unique publication, which goal is to provide accessible information about our country that will allow both Kazakhstanis and foreigners to learn a lot about Kazakhstan, culture and its history. We also hope you will make amazing discoveries about unique sights, natural landscapes and a great desire will spark to visit these amazing places of history, culture and geography.
Works to create this singular publication were conducted for 5 years. This was a lengthy and fundamental project that introduced a three-volume first edition in Kazakh and Russian that provides comprehensive information about Kazakhstan.
The authors of the popular scientific articles of the Atlas are scientists and specialists who have been directly involved in the study of monuments for many years and have made considerable efforts to preserve them. The studies were conducted in five geographic regions of Kazakhstan. Participants of the scientific expeditions literally covered a huge area from Altai to the Caspian Sea to explore the already known and seek new monuments of archeology, architecture and history with the subsequent documentation and ethnographic research.
The publication is designed for different categories of readers: from schoolchildren and students to specialists in the social sciences. Through history, culture and geography of Kazakhstan, many Kazakhstanis will re-discover their way to the heritage of their ancestors and learn more about their country.

title: Guide on admission to the internship and PhD — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
Guide on admission to the internship and PhD
(Қаз) Нұсқаулықта шетелдік Жоғарғы оқу орындарына тағылымдама және Ph.D. алу мақсатымен оқуға түсу процестерінің қыр-сырлары баяндалады. Ұсыным хатының ролі. Ынта таныту эссесін қалай жазу керек? Түйіндемені дайындағанда қандай өзекті жағдайларға мән беру қажет? Шетелдік ЖООның профессорларымен байланысты қалай ұстайды? Және ең бастысы, осындай кезеңдерден нәтижелі өте алған студенттердің ақыл-кеңестері мен ұсыныстарымен таныса аласыз.
The manual details the process of admission for internships and Ph.D. in foreign universities. The role of the letter of recommendation. How to write a motivational essay? What should I look for in my resume? How to communicate with professors of foreign universities? And most importantly, in it you will find advice and recommendations from students who have successfully passed these stages!

title: P.L. Kapitsa "Everything is simple is the truth" — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
P.L. Kapitsa “Everything is simple is the truth”
Originally, the book “All Simple is The Truth” was published on the 100th anniversary of Kapitsa’s birth by the publishing office of the Moscow Physical-Chemical Institute in 1994. Pyotr Evgenyevich Rubinin, Kapitsa’s colleague, acted as the author of the book.
In the history of any science there are names which are known to everybody, even to those who are not especially interested in this science. Isaac Newton and Albert Einstein, Nikolay Lobachevsky and Dmitry Mendeleyev, Shoqan Walikhanov and Kanysh Satpayev – all these are names that remind us of the immortality of genius, of eternal scientific research, human decency, and civil courage.
Among these names is the name of Pyotr Leonidovich Kapitsa (1894-1984). One of the greatest physicists of the 20th century, he lived a long and difficult life, navigating the storms of his era while maintaining his morals, devotion to his friends and his eternal optimism.
The book is designed for a wide range of readers, from high school and college students to professionals in various areas of science, which makes it a sought-for piece. The publication will be of a great interest to many Kazakhstanis familiar with P.L.Kapitsa and his works.

title: Physics in Comics — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
Physics in Comics
Physics is all around us! Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation collaborated with Khan Comics art-studio is publishing the first Kazakhstani action comic book, where the main characters are acquainted with everyday physics in adventures. Comics are oriented to teenagers and 6+ children, but they will also be interesting for adults.
If you always understood why different time zones exist, but did not think about what they might mean, then you will like the comic. We are to publish 10 comics in Kazakh and Russian, with 2,000 copies of an each issue of the comic. The comic is based on the work of the great science popularizer Yakov Perelman, “Zanimatelnaia Fizika”. Yakov Perelman’s work is very relevant, and for our part, we visualized the information in his works in order to meet the requirements of the present time and graphically understand information. Our main message in comics is the presence of physics and the laws of nature in everyday life and we want to demonstrate that physics is primarily around us, and not just in textbooks.
Comic book characters – four teenagers – explore the world around them, visit the sights of Kazakhstan, and help Physics, the hero of the first comic book story arc, restore balance in nature. Each hero has hiw own unique character, skills, strengths and weaknesses. You can purchase comics in all major bookstores in Kazakhstan.

title: Scientific Conferences — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
Scientific Conferences
International Scientific and Practical Conference “Minerageny of Kazakhstan”. 2017
This year on September 21-22 there has been the International Scientific and Practical Conference “Minerageny of Kazakhstan” organized in Almaty in the conference hall of the K. I. Satbayev Institute of Geological Sciences, which was dedicated to the 90th anniversary of the birth of a prominent scientist and production organizer, a well-known statesman, educator of engineering workforce and academician Shakhmardan Yessenov.
The conference was held under the auspices of the Ministry of Investment and Development of the Republic of Kazakhstan, the Ministry of Energy of the Republic of Kazakhstan, the Ministry of Education and Science of the Republic of Kazakhstan, as well as the Committee of Geology and Mineral Resources of the Republic of Kazakhstan.
Organizers of the conference: Shakhmardan Yessenov Science and Education Foundation, Academy of Mineral Resources of the Republic of Kazakhstan, K. I. Satbayev Institute of Geological Sciences, Satbayev University.
“Science on Earthquakes and their Risks in Central Asia” Almaty, September 2016
General objectives:
- to address the modern understanding of the nature of earthquakes, the threats coming from them, the state of current work and applied techniques for studying active tectonics in Central Asia;
- to create a forum for discussing various strategies aimed at mitigating earthquake risks;
- to establish effective communications between science, the public and government institutions in order to develop and implement appropriate policies.
Specific objectives:
- to promote the development of the science on earthquakes by way of sharing research results and modern research techniques;
- to inspire and encourage earthquake researchers in Kazakhstan;
- to raise awareness among the public and officials responsible for public safety about realistic strategies to reduce earthquake risks.
Venue: K. I. Satbayev Institute of Geological Sciences (Kabanbay Batyr street on the corner of С. Valikhanov street, 69/94)
This conference is supported by the Shakhmardan Yessenov Science and Education Foundation having a long tradition of encouraging geological studies and related education in Kazakhstan. Among the co-hosts of the conference are the Institute of Seismology of the Republic of Kazakhstan, the National Research Technical University of the Republic of Kazakhstan and the project “Earthquakes Without Borders” * (Great Britain).
* The project “Earthquakes Without Borders” has been organized by scientists from British universities and other institutions and represents partnership between independent earthquake researchers and social scientists in countries that make up the large earthquake-prone belt from Italy to China. One of the objectives of the project is to increase the level of development of the science on earthquakes and mitigate risks related thereto by way of sharing knowledge, information, techniques and research data between partner countries ( http://ewf.nerc.ac.uk/).

title: Socrates — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
Socrates
This is a book about Socrates – a man whose contribution to the history of knowledge is truly invaluable. Paradoxically, although his name is known to everyone, there are few reliable facts about his life and teachings. All that we know is contained in the writings of his students or writers of subsequent eras. He himself did not leave a single line. Therefore, it is so difficult to separate the myths that formed over two and a half thousand years around Socrates, from its historical appearance. In this book, the authors tell about this and the other. And about a man who lived a bright life, filled with intellectual and very real adventures. And about the legendary sage who taught people the most important thing – the ability to ask questions correctly. That is, the ability to think properly.
We would not know anything about Socrates, were it not for his two most devoted disciples, the great philosopher Plato, who told of Teacher as a thinker, and the brave warrior, talented strategist Xenophon, who draws Teacher from the personal side. They are also full-fledged heroes of this book, telling not only about Socrates, but also about an entire era – perhaps the most important epoch in the history of Western thought.

title: The Secrets of Usturt — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
The Secrets of Usturt
The book is based on materials from the “The Secrets of Usturt” expedition, which was initiated by Margulan Seisembai. Over 250 travelers in 97 SUV’s, in love with the nature of their native land, traveled around the peninsula in 7 days, visited its legendary natural and man-made monuments, met hospitable residents of the region.
We hope that the book will also help you discover Mangystau, and you will want to come here yourself. The guide contains photo cards that can be sent by mail, expedition map, travel tips and brief descriptions of attractions. The publication used high-quality photographs of expedition members.
The guide “The Secrets of Usturt” was released in 1000 copies, in Kazakh, English and Russian. A gift version is also available.

Shakhmardan Yessenov Scholarship 2026
PLEASE READ CAREFULLY THE PROGRAM PROVISIONS AT THE BOTTOM OF THE PAGE.
Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation announces a competition for the provision of 20 monthly student scholarships 70,000 tenge each.
Program participants: students, citizens of Kazakhstan over 18, studying at universities of Kazakhstan in 2-3 years of studying of a bachelor’s degree programme, as well as 2-5 year undergraduate courses for medical schools, in natural science, technical, medical and IT specialties.
Number of scholarships: 20
To participate in the Competition for the Shakhmardan Yessenov scholarship, the candidate must:
- Fill in an online application form on the Foundation’s website – yessenovfoundation.org
- Attach to the application form:
Required documents:
- ID scan;
- Essay titled “Is it easy to become a professional in the modern world? What hinders and what contributes to this?” (text in the edited document);
- The transcript of records of the student (GPA no less than 3.0). *Аpplications with the score below 3.0 are not considered;
- A certificate of no criminal record/ criminal record.
Additional documents:
- Science certificates/diplomas – no more than 2 certificates*;
- Social activities and volunteering certificates – no more than three (3) certificates*;
- Publications – no more than two (2) publications*;
- Recommendation letter from the scientific supervisor/head of department – no more than one (1) document.
*The Foundation considers limited number of certificates. A candidate has to independently select certificates, which best reflect his/her achievements.
Applications are accepted on the Foundation’s website yessenovfoundation.org from February 2 through March 3, 2026.
 The deadline for acceptance of applications is March 3, 2026, 3:00 P.M. (GMT+5). Applications received after the deadline will not be considered.

text:
Yessenov Scholarship 2019
Academic Shakhmardan Yessenov Science and Education Foundation announces a competition for 25 monthly student scholarships. The grant is awarded for 12 months and amounts to 25 000 tenge.
Who can participate: citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan who study at Kazakhstani universities on 2-3 courses of Bachelor’s degree or 1 year Master’s degree in geology, IT, medical, technical and engineering specialties.
Number of grants: 25
Required documents:
- application form (available on the website of the Foundation yessenovfoundation.org);

Deadline for submission of documents: until 24.02.2019. All applications must be sent to info@yessenovfoundation.org

Research Internships in Laboratories for Graduate Students and Specialists 2018 — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
Research Internships in Laboratories for Graduate Students and Specialists 2018
Provision of 5 grants to the graduate students and/or specialists with major in natural sciences to undertake a research internship in laboratories around the world with the subsequent opportunity of applying to a Ph.D program in the future (undertaken by the students themselves).
Program eligibility
The Program is open to (a) working research specialists with a degree no higher than Master’s, (b) undergraduate students in their senior year and (c) graduate students (both 1st and 2nd year) majoring in engineering and natural sciences and enrolled in universities of Kazakhstan. Applicants must be citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan.
Number of grants: 5
Grant amount: 7000 US Dollars.
Required documents:
- CV;
- Scan copy of applicant’s national ID;
- Scan copy of applicant’s official transcript and bachelor degree diploma (for graduate students and working specialists);
- Copies of diplomas, certificates, scientific projects, etc.
- An essay on the subject: “I would like pursue to science because…”
- Recommendation letter(s) from research advisor
Application call is open from Jan 8 through Feb 18, 2018
Application and all the required documents must be sent to the Foundation’s e-mail: info@yessenovfoundation.org.

Yessenov Data Lab 2018 — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
Yessenov Data Lab 2018
The Yessenov Data Lab is an 8-week long intensive summer school that fast launches into the Data Scientist career to solve challenges that various businesses face. Upon completion of the program, the participants will receive a Data Scientist certificate.
Dates of the school: Jun 11 – Aug 3, 2018
 Schedule: Mon-Fri 9am-6pm
 Cohort: 20 participants
 Venue: AlmaU, Almaty
The graduates of the summer school can look for to acquire the following skills:
- Programming in Python within data analysis
- Preprocessing
- Visualization of data and finding data dependency
- Forecasting on the basis of historical data
- Understanding different algorithms of training
- Right choice of model of training
- Fundamental understanding of functioning of neural networks
Program eligibility
Applicants must be a Kazakhstani citizen (over 18), student (undergraduate, graduate or Ph.D.) or practicing specialist with strong analytical skills and basic knowledge in linear algebra and statistics.
Requirements to the applicants:
- Strong analytical skills
- Basic knowledge of statistics and linear algebra
- The following is a plus:- Programming skills;
- Knowledge of source control (git/svn/…), command line/ssh, testing, developer methodologies (Agile/spiral/…), cloud familiarity (AWS/Azure/…);
- Certificates of successful completion of programming courses or a bachelor diploma in Science in CS or other tech discipline (math, physics, and engineering);
- Upper intermediate English (6.5 IELTS or 90 TOEFL ibt or other way to show fluency).
 
Number of grants: 20
Winners will undertake a course on a gratuitous basis. The participants residing outside of Almaty will be provided with financial sponsorship that covers a two-way domestic flight to Almaty, accommodation and daily meals on ground or a cash equivalent of the listed expenses for the period of the Program.
Instruction language: Russian with occasional usage of technical English.
Required documents:
- Copy of ID;
- Copy of diplomas, certificates on completion of courses (programming, statistics, etc.), participation in loympiads (math, IT or any other tech disciplines);
- Copy of transcript (all completed semesters) and a copy of bachelor degree diploma with transcript (for graduates and specialists);
- Essay on “I want to learn data analysis to…” (not less than 7 sentences and no more than 2 pages);
- Detailed portfolio demonstrating achievements in IT field (where possible);

title: Yessenov Data Lab 2019 — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation

Yessenov Data Lab 2019
The Yessenov Data Lab is an 8-week long intensive summer school program that fast tracks its participants into a Data Science career, and provides a unique opportunity to solve real-life business challenges. Upon the completion of this program, participants will receive a Data Scientist certificate.
Dates of the school: Jun 10 – Aug 2, 2018.Schedule: Mon-Fri 9am-6pm
Cohort: 25 participants. Venue: AlmaU, Almaty
The graduates of the summer school will be acquiring the following skills:
- Programming in Python within data analysis
- Preprocessing
- Visualization of data and finding data dependency
- Forecasting on the basis of historical data
- Understanding different algorithms of training
- Right choice of model of training
- Fundamental understanding of functioning of neural networks
Program eligibility
Applicants must be citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan (over 18), students (undergraduate, graduate or Ph.D.) or practicing specialists with strong analytical skills and basic knowledge in linear algebra and statistics
Requirements to candidates:

- The following is a plus:
- Programming skills;

Internships in IT startups 2018
“INTERNSHIPS IN IT STARTUPS IN USA” COMPETITION
Does experiencing the startup life in Silicon Valley sound like dream? Do you have what it takes to succeed in the high-tech start-up capital of the world? The Yessenov Foundation gives you the unique possibility to prove yourself. Yessenov Foundation is launching a competition for a free internship at Sillicon Valley’s most promising startups.
All citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan are eligible to enter the competition. The main criteria for selection are your professional skills and desire to self-improve. It doesn’t matter who you are right now – an accomplished IT professional or a Master’s student – what matters is who you want to become!
The “Internships in IT startups in USA” program is your chance to drastically change your life!

title: Chess at School — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
Chess at School
The project consisted in introducing chess as a subject into the school curriculum and teaching it in experimental classes of 18 schools in Almaty, Kyzylorda, Pavlodar and Pavlodar Region. It also included a study on the psychodiagnostics of the cognitive development of children who studied chess under the project in comparison with children from control classes where the subject was not taught. The project was carried out by the Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation in conjunction with the Kazakhstan Chess Federation for the second, third and fourth grades.
The study results showed that the pupils who studied chess demonstrated a higher level of intelligence, creativity and academic performance after three years in comparison with their peers who did not study the game. In the three years in which the Chess at School programme was implemented, the foundation provided 58 secondary schools in Kazakhstan with equipment for chess rooms; 18 schools received printed textbooks, while the rest got electronic versions. A report on the results of the programme was submitted to the Ministry of Education and Science of Kazakhstan for the introduction of chess as a variable subject into the curricula of the country’s secondary schools.
Results of the Chess at School project. News

title: Chess Promotion in Kazakhstan — Shakhmardan Yessenov Foundation
text:
Chess Promotion in Kazakhstan
The updated and improved web resource of the Kazakhstan Chess Federation (KCF) was launched on January 18. The design of the resource, kazchess.kz, has been completely renovated to a new pattern reflecting the latest global tendencies in user interface and the structure is simplified to enhance the user experience.
In the process of updating the website, feedback both from the chess players and the Federation’s management was taken into consideration, and resulted in a new main menu and an optimized content. Additionally, the new web site has the ability to embed any module, whereas the old one did not have such function available for the administrator.
Moreover, there is now the possibility of online broadcast of chess matches from any competition held under the auspices of the KCF. The website has also been adjusted for an appropriate display on any computers and become “mobile ready”. The visitors will be able to bind his or her comments of the web-site with their social media accounts. Upgraded calendar of events and ratings of chess players was also added.
As the KCF Executive Director Irina Grishchenko said, “We are confident that the new website fully meets all the latest requirements, and will allow us to strengthen ties with members of the Federation, professional chess players and to increase the number of chess lovers in Kazakhstan, contributing to the popularization of the game within the country”.
The renovation of KCF’s online resource has been carried out by Neolabs, a web-studio with a 15-year old experience in designing architecture and infrastructure of web services. Amongts the works of the studio there are web products for various educational projects including The Shahmardan Yessenov Foundation and Global Student Center and companies such as ASTEL and Komesk-Omir. The Federation’s website is currently being finalized, and we would be happy to hear your opinion on its structure and functioning. By visiting the website and testing its work, you can send your suggestions to us: office@kazchess.kz.
